summary: Matched the Raikou and Suicune item translation units by replacing their 345-line assembly files with C implementations, marking both objects as Matching, and wiring their C object files into the build. The PR also generalized Entei's special-attributes typedef to itPokeDogsAttributes because Entei, Raikou, and Suicune share the same f32 plus timer layout.
what_changed: Deleted the old hand-assembly item implementations for itraikou.s and itsuikun.s.

Added 164-line C implementations for src/melee/it/items/itraikou.c and src/melee/it/items/itsuikun.c, including state tables, spawn/setup routines, physics callbacks, hitlag effect callbacks, accessory callbacks, and state-transition wrappers.
Switched configure.py entries for melee/it/items/itraikou.c and melee/it/items/itsuikun.c from NonMatching to Matching.
Replaced obj_files.mk references to asm/melee/it/items/itraikou.s.o and asm/melee/it/items/itsuikun.s.o with src/melee/it/items/itraikou.c.o and src/melee/it/items/itsuikun.c.o.
Renamed the shared special-attributes struct in src/melee/it/itCommonItems.h from itEnteiAttributes to itPokeDogsAttributes, preserving fields f32 x0 and s32 timer.
Updated itentei.c to use itPokeDogsAttributes instead of itEnteiAttributes, showing the same attribute layout applies across the legendary dog Pokemon items.
Expanded itraikou.h and itsuikun.h prototypes and changed header includes from it/forward.h to platform.h plus it/item.h.
smart_moves: Matched Raikou and Suicune together because their assembly structure is nearly parallel: each has the same state-table shape, timer-driven SFX loop, effect-spawn accessory callback, and common ground/air state handling.
Generalized the attribute typedef rather than duplicating separate Entei/Raikou/Suicune attribute types with the same layout, reducing future churn when other Pokemon dog code is touched.
Kept item-specific constants local to each C file: Raikou uses effect 0x46D and SFX 0x2745-0x2748, while Suicune uses effect 0x469 and SFX 0x2749-0x274C.
Preserved matching-sensitive stack shape with PAD_STACK(16) in init functions and PAD_STACK(8) in camera/effect helper routines.

Used existing item helpers and callbacks directly instead of open-coding behavior: Item_80268E5C, Item_8026AE84, it_80272C6C, it_80272860, it_8026E248, it_8026D62C, it_8027A118, efLib_PauseAll, efLib_ResumeAll, efLib_DestroyAll, and efSync_Spawn.
decomp_lessons: When multiple items share identical special-attribute data, prefer a neutral typedef name. This PR renamed itEnteiAttributes to itPokeDogsAttributes once Raikou and Suicune also consumed the layout.
For Pokemon item families, compare sibling assembly files side by side; many functions differ mainly by symbol addresses, effect IDs, and SFX IDs.
After replacing item assembly with C, update both configure.py matching status and obj_files.mk object paths or the build may still use the old asm object.
State tables can be expressed directly as ItemStateTable arrays with two entries matching the assembly .data layout: one action-state entry and one -1 terminator/alternate-state entry.
Accessory callbacks can mirror itcmd flag behavior by checking xDAC_itcmd_var0, clearing it, setting xDB4_itcmd_var2, spawning the effect, and starting the initial SFX.
Timer loops in item per-frame callbacks can be matched by decrementing xDD4_itemVar.entei.timer, selecting an SFX with HSD_Randi(3), playing it, and restoring the timer from special attributes.
naming_conventions: Shared legendary-dog item attributes are named itPokeDogsAttributes, not itEnteiAttributes, even though the item variable storage path remains xDD4_itemVar.entei.timer.
Unidentified functions remain address-based, e.g. it_802CF814, it_802CFC48, it_802CF978, it_802CFDAC.
Global item state tables retain address-based names: it_803F7DE8 for Raikou and it_803F7E08 for Suicune.
Header guards remain address-derived: GALE01_2CF814 for itraikou.h and GALE01_2CFC48 for itsuikun.h.
The C files use project item typedefs such as Item_GObj, Item, Article, HSD_GObj, HSD_JObj, and ItemStateTable.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(16) in the initialization functions and PAD_STACK(8) in routines whose assembly required extra stack space.
Used GET_ITEM((HSD_GObj*) gobj) in functions where that form matched generated code, while using gobj->user_data directly in others.
Represented trivial assembly functions as empty void functions with an explicit return.
Matched branch/state behavior with bool returns: per-frame callbacks return true when it_80272C6C reports false, otherwise false; transition callbacks return false after ground/air handling.
Used a switch on HSD_Randi(3) for the three randomized follow-up SFX values, matching the assembly branch table/compare pattern.
Kept effect-spawn scale as local f32 v = 1.0f passed by address to efSync_Spawn, corresponding to the deleted sdata2 0x3F800000 constants.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename itPokeDogsAttributes fields x0 and timer or clarify xDD4_itemVar.entei reuse.
Search sibling Pokemon item PRs for the same ItemStateTable and accessory-callback matching pattern.
Check whether itraikou/itsuikun function names were later given semantic names beyond address-based symbols.
